column66: Ran out of food and not enough variety of talks
Two (explicit) poster sessions would have been cool.
Fantastic ! cannot wait for #CBIAS2023
For me there were not enough talks focused on software and too much panels. I would add more science and lower these panel discussions.

I loved being able to attend virtually and in person - as a person with a health condition it was very useful to have that flexibility.
The amount of vegetarian food available at the evening social would be my only complaint. I understand it was all put out at the beginning of the night (without any sign indicating it was for vegetarians) so it got eaten by everyone before the veggies had a chance to get a decent portion - a mistake on the part of the venue most likely!
It would be great if the recordings of the talks were shared with meeting participants
Felt pretty disconnected from the conference. Had to pay registration fee out of own pocket.
Thank you for the amazing time! For the lunch breaks the space might have been a bit too limited - more tables would have made things more comfortable for eating.
Question & Answers for TechBites would really nice.

It would have been great to be able to see the posters online and to ask questions. Also, a couple of speakers were not speaking on the microphone and not using the cursor to point. Please do not give those people a cordless mic and laser pointer. Rather, they should speak to the microphone in front of the computer and use the cursor to point. But overall, I was very satisfied with the online experience, worth getting up at 3am! I would not have been able to attend in person, so thank you for giving this opportunity, much appreciated. Good job!!

Would have been nice to have dinner a bit earlier on the first day, or have it clearly stated that dinner would be at 8 and not during the drinks reception, I had evening plans so unfortunately wasn’t able to stay.
needed more space for the posters (was quite crowded)
Not enough good speakers, looked liked friends inviting friends.

Speakers could have had wireless microphones to be able to move around. 
There were too little tables for lunch. 
The poster space was very small, especially the distance between the posters made it hard to look through all of them. Would also be nice to have a short poster session on the second day. 

Thanks for organising a great event!
Make posters available for online attendants.
I don't use slack, maybe point this out before the conference so that I download the app.
It was really interesting and well organised, perfection in all the details!

Some of the presenter and questions were impossible to hear because the speakers didn't talk into the microphone. It would had been great if a clip-on microphone had been used instead of the desk microphone.
excellent to provide an online/virtual access, would have loved to see the posters as well but understand that this is hard to implement

I really enjoyed the commercial talks, and I think they should occupy a larger space. They are trying to make effort to be open and showcase what they worked on in Image analysis field, and I rely on some of those softwares, I would have liked to see more of what they are working on. Otherwise, the symposium was great but only very few questions from the online chat was asked or replied, even after the symposium. It would be great if it could be mandatory for the speaker to sit in front of zoom after their talk to write answers to the chat. Kudos to the organizers, that was great.
Slack wasn't working on my phone, but I couldn't access wifi anyway

1- It could be better to have more time for questions after long talks. A lot of things were mentioned in long talks, but we didn't have enough time to ask questions.
2- It would be better to use headset microphones to have a smooth high quality of voice. A traditional microphone is not good for speakers who move their hands a lot.

The room for the poster session was very crowded - a bit more space in future would be appreciated. 
Other than that, it was a very enjoyable symposium - I look forward to the next one!
The posters were too close to each other, making it hard to read them and talk to the presenter without blocking the path/getting in each other's way.

Please, have more vegan food options. Non-vegans eat the vegan food if it's not separated, and then vegans end up with nothing. It's nice to give people options, but please consider ordering more vegan food to account for the non-vegans also eating it.

It was a fantastic conference from a sponsor point of view. The exhibition space was excellent from our point of view being so integrated with the auditorium and coffee/food. We had some really good interactions as a result of that.
Well done guys - really good event.

The talks were nicely varied. I was so happy to interact with other facility staff and see people in person. I could speak to them about which of the tools that were presented or that are out there are actually useful on a small facility level where there is not enough data to warrant dedicated machine learning approaches. I certainly missed this opportunity last year during the online only event so very happy with it having been in person. 

I was impressed how many international speakers and attendees there were. 

Mugs instead of cups for Tea would be nice - they were just too small :) 
More fruit instead of biscuits on tea/coffee breaks. 
Lunch on the second day was a bit sparse. I was lucky to have had a big breakfast at the hotel in the morning.

A headphone for some of the more “active” speakers would have been desirable.
I was unable to attend to all the talks, so a recording of them would have also been great.
Finally, I could not figure out how to look up the posters, probably there was a way, but I could not see them.
Looking forward to attending live next week.
